name: Szechwan Cucumber Salad
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Small dried wood ear mushrooms
4 red hot peppers, cut into thin rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour boiling water over the tree ears and let stand for 15 minutes.
2. Peel the cucumbers, cut them in half lengthwise, and scrape out the seeds.
3. Slice the cucumbers into thin half-moons.
4. When the mushrooms are soft, drain, rinse, and dry them.
5. Combine the sliced mushrooms, cucumbers, remaining ingredients (soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, salt, sesame oil), and let stand for 15 minutes.
6. Toss again before serving.
NOTE: Wood ears can be found in Chinese markets. After soaking, they have little taste but lots of crunch. They're there for the texture. Dried duck web or jellyfish could be substituted, but wood ears are more commonly available. Use dark, cooked Asian sesame oil instead of light sesame oil for better flavor.
